summary: Decompiled the Fire Flower item module by replacing asm/melee/it/items/itfflower.s with a matching C implementation in src/melee/it/items/itfflower.c, wiring it into the build as Matching, and adding the needed headers, item-var struct, prototypes, and symbol-scope updates. The PR also tightened related declarations such as it_8026DF34 returning bool and ithammer's analogous transition helper returning bool.
what_changed: Deleted the 766-line assembly file asm/melee/it/items/itfflower.s and added a 357-line C implementation at src/melee/it/items/itfflower.c.
Marked melee/it/items/itfflower.c as Object(Matching, ...) in configure.py and swapped obj_files.mk from the asm object to the C object.
Implemented the Fire Flower item state table it_803F6090 and functions covering spawn/setup, state transitions, physics/collision callbacks, flame emission, and helper callbacks.
Added FFlowerVars to itCommonItems.h with fields x0 and x4, then added FFlowerVars fflower to Item.xDD4_itemVar in src/melee/it/types.h.
Moved the declaration for it_80292D48 out of the broad it_27CF.h header and into itfflower.h; it_26B1.c now includes it/items/itfflower.h where that declaration is needed.
Added public itfflower.h prototypes for it_80292D48, it_80292E64, it_80292EAC, it_80292EF8, and it_80292F14.
Added the itlgunbeam.h prototype for it_802996D0(HSD_GObj*, s32, u32, f32), which itfflower.c calls when firing flame/beam behavior.
Changed it_8026DF34's declaration in it_266F.h from s32 to bool and adjusted ithammer.c's similar it_802940B8 return type and false return to match.

Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt so internal itfflower functions such as it_80292FF0, it_80293040, it_8029310C, it_80293110, it_8029313C, it_80293164, it_80293210, it_80293240, it_802932AC, it_802933C0, it_80293414, it_80293444, it_8029355C, it_80293608, and it_8029360C are local scope instead of global.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt so local float constants it_804DCB00, it_804DCB04, it_804DCB08, and it_804DCB0C are marked local rather than global.
smart_moves: Kept helper callbacks static in C and reflected that by marking their symbols local, reducing unnecessary global surface area after decompilation.
Introduced a small module-local helper, msid_check(Item_GObj* gobj, Item* ip), to share repeated msid/effect-cleanup logic across multiple callbacks instead of duplicating the switch each time.
Used the existing item variable union pattern by adding FFlowerVars to xDD4_itemVar rather than leaving raw xDD4/xDD8 offset accesses in the new C.
Preserved matching-sensitive details visible in the C such as PAD_STACK(4), explicit false returns from bool callbacks, and modulo-by-6 state cycling for xDD4_itemVar.fflower.x0.
Kept the second parameter of it_80292F14 as s32 because the callee declaration for it_802996D0 requires that type, as noted by the PR author.
Improved header ownership by including itfflower.h where it_80292D48 is actually used instead of keeping the Fire Flower spawn prototype in the generic it_27CF.h header.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling an item module, update all three integration points together: remove the asm object, add the C object in obj_files.mk, and mark the source Object(Matching, ...) in configure.py.
After converting asm helpers to C static functions, update symbols.txt scopes from global to local for functions and file-local constants to keep symbol metadata consistent with C linkage.
Item-specific xDD4 storage should be named through an item-var struct in itCommonItems.h and the Item.xDD4_itemVar union rather than accessed as anonymous offsets.
Use existing item state-table conventions: local/static callback functions can populate a global ItemStateTable, while externally called routines remain declared in the module header.
Return types should follow semantic use and callee contracts; this PR changed it_8026DF34 and an analogous ithammer helper to bool when they behaved as boolean callbacks.
Header cleanup matters during decompilation: move declarations from broad catch-all headers into the specific item header and include that item header at use sites.
Matching C may need small helper factoring plus stack padding rather than a direct one-function-one-assembly-block transcription, as long as generated code still matches.
naming_conventions: Item-specific variable structs use a descriptive item prefix plus Vars suffix, e.g. FFlowerVars.
Item.xDD4_itemVar union members use lower-case item names, e.g. fflower.
Unidentified fields remain offset-style placeholders such as x0 and x4 when evidence for semantic names is not yet available.
Unresolved functions keep address-based names such as it_80292F14 and it_802996D0, with address comments in headers.
Internal item callbacks can remain address-named but be declared static in the C file and local in symbols.txt.
Boolean item callbacks should use bool and return false rather than int and 0 when the surrounding item API expects boolean semantics.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted the Fire Flower spawn routine it_80292D48 into C using a local SpawnItem, explicit initialization of kind, position, facing_dir, velocity, parent fields, and flag bit x44_flag.bits.b0 before calling Item_80268B18.
Matched zero and constant float behavior through explicit 0.0F, -1.0F, 5.5F, and 2.5F assignments, replacing former local sdata2 constants.
Used PAD_STACK(4) in callbacks such as it_80293040 and it_802932AC where stack shape needed preservation.
Kept it_80292F14's second argument as s32 to match it_802996D0's prototype, despite the name arg1 providing little semantic evidence.
Used a modulo operation, ip->xDD4_itemVar.fflower.x0 %= 6, matching the assembly's multiply-high modulo-by-6 sequence.
Factored repeated msid cleanup into an inline helper while still matching the behavior of effect destruction and xDD4_itemVar.fflower.x4 reset.
Changed local functions and constants to local symbol scope after replacing assembly globals with C static declarations.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or submitted reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
Author note: the declaration for it_80292D48 was removed from it_27CF.h and replaced by including itfflower.h at its use site.
Author note: the second parameter of it_80292F14 was intentionally left as s32 because it is passed to a function declared with that type.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename FFlowerVars fields x0 and x4 to semantic names.
Find later PRs touching it_80292F14 or it_802996D0 to clarify the s32 argument meaning.
Check whether Fire Flower callbacks it_80293040, it_80293164, it_802932AC, and it_8029355C received semantic names after this PR.
Search for other item decomp PRs that moved declarations out of it_27CF.h into item-specific headers.
Search for item PRs that changed symbols.txt function scopes from global to local after static C conversion.
